title: NO GOTION Act
plain_summary: The NO GOTION Act is a new law that stops certain companies from getting tax breaks for green energy projects. If a company is owned by or works closely with countries that are considered enemies of the United States, they cannot receive these special tax benefits. The law explains exactly which types of companies and relationships with foreign countries would make a business unable to get these tax breaks. This is meant to prevent unfriendly foreign governments from benefiting from American green energy programs.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the No Official Giveaways Of Taxpayers' Income to Oppressive Nations Act or the NO GOTION Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Denial of Green Energy Tax Benefits to Companies Associated with Foreign Adversaries
sections.text: This section changes the tax code to stop certain companies from getting green energy tax benefits. Companies that cannot get these benefits are called disqualified companies. A disqualified company is any business that falls into one of these groups. First, companies that are owned or controlled by foreign adversary governments. This includes the government itself, government agencies, and any company that the government owns or controls. It also includes any company that is organized under the laws of a foreign adversary country or has its headquarters there. Second, companies that are owned, controlled, directed, or influenced by foreign adversary parties. This includes any company where foreign adversary parties own at least ten percent of the stock or have ten percent of voting rights or board control. It also includes companies that are controlled or influenced by foreign adversary parties in their actions, management, or operations. Third, companies with debt or other business arrangements with foreign adversary parties that give those parties influence over the company or provide substantial benefits to them. These arrangements include debt, leases, management contracts, manufacturing contracts, license agreements, and financial derivatives. However, simply buying equipment or materials in a normal business deal does not count as providing substantial benefit. The term control means the same thing as defined in federal regulations about foreign investment. Foreign adversary means the same countries listed in federal defense law as covered nations, plus Cuba and Venezuela while Nicholas Maduro is president. The Treasury Secretary can issue guidance on how to enforce these rules, including how to determine ownership percentages for publicly traded companies and how to prevent companies from avoiding these requirements. These changes apply to tax years that begin after this law is passed.
